name: Desktop App Surface
description: Rules for workflows executed in a native desktop application, including window focus, document state, local dialogs, and save/export flows.
whenToApply: The phase happens in a native macOS or desktop application.
The agent must inspect app windows, edit local content, use app menus, save files, or export artifacts through a desktop UI.
whenNotToApply: The phase happens in a browser tab or web application.
The phase is only terminal command execution.
generationGuidance: Require the phase to confirm the active app, active document or window, and whether the agent is editing an original or a new copy.
Require validation after save, export, import, destructive edit, or document state changes.
Add user approval before overwriting user files, deleting content, sending from an app, or changing shared/local settings.
Stop when the target app, window, document, permission dialog, or save/export result cannot be observed.
hardRequirements: Every desktop app phase must include the expected app/window/document state.
Every overwrite, destructive edit, or shared-location save must include an approval checkpoint.

runtimeReference.title: Desktop App Surface Rules
runtimeReference.purpose: Desktop app execution rules and common local UI automation mistakes.
whenToRead: Before executing any phase that uses a native desktop app.
